subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: dy/dx = y²/(x-1) — Slope Field, Tangent Line Approx & Separation of Variables

parts.prompt: On the axes provided, sketch a slope field for the given differential equation at the six points indicated.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Correct zero slopes at points where $y = 0$
1 pt: Correct nonzero slopes at remaining points (slopes positive where $\frac{y^2}{x-1} > 0$, negative where $x < 1$ and $y \ne 0$)
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Zero slopes at all points on the $x$-axis (where $y = 0$). Nonzero slopes must be correctly signed based on $\frac{dy}{dx} = \frac{y^2}{x-1}$.

parts.prompt: Let $y = f(x)$ be the particular solution to the given differential equation with the initial condition $f(2) = 3$. Write an equation for the line tangent to the graph of $y = f(x)$ at $x = 2$. Use your equation to approximate $f(2.1)$.
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: Slope at $(2, 3)$ is $\frac{3^2}{2-1} = 9$. Tangent line: $y = 3 + 9(x-2)$. Approximation: $f(2.1) \approx 3.9$.

parts.prompt: Find the particular solution $y = f(x)$ to the given differential equation with the initial condition $f(2) = 3$.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Uses initial condition $f(2) = 3$: $-\frac{1}{3} = \ln(1) + C \Rightarrow C = -\frac{1}{3}$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $-\frac{1}{y} = \ln|x-1| + C$. Using $f(2)=3$: $C = -\frac{1}{3}$. Final answer: $y = f(x) = \frac{1}{\frac{1}{3} - \ln(x-1)}$, valid for $1 < x < 1 + e^{1/3}$. 0/5 if no separation; max 3/5 if no constant of integration.
